q: Which kind of 3D printing came first? It was patented in the mid-1980s and builds a part by using a beam of light to harden liquid resin, one thin layer at a time.
Which 3D printing plastic is made mostly from plants like corn, breaks down more easily than most plastics, and is the usual first material for beginners?

For decades, almost every model has been saved in one simple file type before printing. It stores only the shape, as a mesh of triangles — no color, size, or material. What is this long-standing standard file?
Which family of 3D printing makes strong metal parts — like jet-engine and rocket pieces — by spreading a thin layer of metal powder and melting it with a laser or electron beam?
Most home printers can't handle it. Which high-performance plastic needs a nozzle near 400°C and a heated chamber, and is tough enough for aerospace parts and medical implants?
Cheap home 3D printers suddenly appeared around 2009, right after a key patent ran out. Which method — squeezing melted plastic through a moving nozzle — was finally opened up to everyone?
Which common 3D printing material is soft and rubbery instead of hard, so makers use it to print phone cases, gaskets, and watch straps that bend?

A newer printing file type, backed by several big tech companies, was built to fix the old standard's gaps. Unlike that older file, it can store color, materials, and several parts together. What is this modern format?
Which 3D printing process never melts anything? It builds parts by spraying a liquid glue onto a bed of powder, layer by layer — handy for full-color models and for sand molds used in metal casting.

The file your 3D printer actually reads while printing isn't the 3D model. It's a long list of exact movement and temperature commands — the same language that runs many factory cutting machines. What is it called?
Croatia's biggest city sits inland. Which city is the largest one that sits right on the seacoast?
Of all the islands along Croatia's coast, which one has the most people living on it year-round?
Which mountain holds the highest peak found anywhere in Croatia?
Which river runs the longest distance across Croatia, counting only the part inside the country?
Croatia touches five countries by land. Which neighbor does it share its longest land border with?
Croatia has eight national parks. Which one draws the most visitors each year?
Which is the largest natural lake in Croatia?
Which of Croatia's well-known cities lies the farthest south?
Croatia is split into 20 counties plus its capital. Which county covers the most land, yet has the fewest people of them all?
Croatia's northwest is taken up by one big, roughly triangular peninsula — also the largest in the whole Adriatic Sea. Which peninsula is it?
Of all the state capitals in the connected 48 states, which one sits the farthest north?
Three states are bigger than this one, but every one of those three touches an ocean. What is the largest state with no ocean coast?
Every state has a single highest point. In one state, that highest point is lower than the highest point of any other state. Which state is it?
Setting aside Alaska and Hawaii, which state was the last to join the United States?
Which state has the fewest people living in it?
Several states sit on the Great Lakes. Which state touches the most of the five?
Which state is divided into the fewest counties?

dimension: most populous non-host of the Olympics
q: What is the most populous country in the world that has never hosted the Summer Olympic Games?

dimension: most NFL wins, no Super Bowl
q: Among NFL teams that have never won a Super Bowl, which franchise has piled up the most regular-season wins in its history?
Among countries that have competed in the Eurovision Song Contest but never won it, which has entered the most times?
Which NBA franchise has piled up the most regular-season wins in its history without ever winning a championship?

dimension: largest country never in a World Cup
q: What is the largest country by land area whose men's soccer team has never qualified for a World Cup?

dimension: biggest film franchise, no Oscar nod
q: What is the highest-grossing film franchise that has never received even a single Academy Award nomination?

dimension: most English titles, no European Cup
q: Which English club has won the most English league titles but has never lifted the European Cup or Champions League?

dimension: most populous country, no woman leader
q: What is the most populous country that has never had a woman serve as its head of government or head of state?

dimension: most Best Picture noms, no win
q: Which film studio or distributor has earned the most Best Picture nominations at the Oscars without ever winning the award?
Which country has played in more men's World Cups than any other nation that has never won the tournament?

dimension: largest economy, no permanent UNSC seat
q: What is the largest national economy that has never held a permanent seat on the UN Security Council?

dimension: most foreign-film Oscar noms, no win
q: Which country has been nominated the most times for the Best International Feature Oscar (the foreign-language award) without ever winning it?

dimension: most Cup Finals, no Stanley Cup
q: Among NHL teams that have never won the Stanley Cup, which franchise has reached the Stanley Cup Final the most times?

dimension: most populous country, no one in space
q: What is the most populous country that has never sent one of its own citizens into space?

dimension: biggest animated franchise, no Oscar win
q: What is the highest-grossing animated film franchise that has never won an Academy Award?

dimension: most Women's World Cup podiums, no title
q: Which country has finished on the Women's World Cup podium the most times without ever winning the tournament?
What is the largest country by land area that has never won a medal at the Summer Olympics?
Which band placed more singles at No. 2 on the Billboard Hot 100 than anyone else, while never once reaching No. 1?
Which country has won the most Summer Olympic medals without ever winning a single gold?

dimension: most populous country, no UNESCO site
q: What is the most populous country that does not have a single UNESCO World Heritage Site?
The first thirteen states were the original colonies. Which state was the very next one to join the country after them?
Of all the men actually voted into the presidency, who was the youngest at the time he won?
Which president stood taller than any other when he held the office?
Among the states that have never been the birthplace of a president, which one has the most people living in it today?
Who was president when the stock market collapsed and the Great Depression began?
One state ran itself as its own independent country for almost a decade before it joined the United States. Which state?
James Buchanan led the country in the years just before the Civil War. Who was the president directly before him?
Counting every man who has ever held the office, not just those elected, who was the youngest to become president?
Of the fifty states, which one was the most recent to join the country?
Who was president when federal soldiers were sent to escort Black students into a Little Rock high school?
How many states broke away to form the Confederacy?
Which president struck the deal that roughly doubled the size of the United States overnight?
Who was president when the United States entered the First World War?
Who was president when Japan attacked Pearl Harbor and pulled the country into World War II?
Who was the first president to win the White House, lose it, and then win it back, serving two terms that did not touch?
About how many men signed the Declaration of Independence?
Every early president was born a British subject. Who was the first president actually born as a citizen of the United States?
Who was president when both the Civil Rights Act and the Voting Rights Act were signed into law?
All or part of how many of today's states were carved out of the land bought in the Louisiana Purchase?
Which president held the job for the shortest time, dying after only about a month in office?
More than half of all wild Asian elephants live in one country. Which one?
Of the great apes — gorilla, orangutan, chimpanzee, bonobo, human — which non-human one is the smallest?
How many hearts does an octopus have?
There are eight species of bear. Which is the smallest?
Of Africa's Big Five — lion, leopard, elephant, rhino, buffalo — which kills the most people?
Which U.S. state has the most moose?
Setting Alaska aside, which U.S. state has the most wild wolves?
Which country has the most wild giraffes?
Which penguin species lives the farthest north — the only one that reaches the equator?
How many bones are in a giraffe's neck?
Which U.S. state has the most American bison?
A cow's stomach is divided into how many compartments?
Mountain gorillas live in just three countries. Which one has the most?
Of the eight bears, which is the only one whose diet is almost all plants?
Which great ape is the only one not native to Africa?
Which country has the most wild orangutans?
Roughly two-thirds of the world's polar bears live in one country. Which one?
Which of the eight bears is the only one classed as a marine mammal?
Of Africa's Big Five, which animal has the smallest wild population?
How many toes does an ostrich have on each foot?
Which country is home to more wild tigers than the rest of the world combined?
Wild cats are native to every inhabited continent but one. Which inhabited continent has none of its own?

dimension: number of big cats that can roar
q: Of all the big cats, only some can let out a true roar. How many can?
Most big cats hunt under cover of darkness. Which one is the odd one out, doing most of its hunting in broad daylight?
Which country has more wild lions than any other?
On which continent do more kinds of wild cat live than anywhere else?
The world's rarest big cat hangs on in the snowy forests of just one country's far east. Which country?
Every brand-new Brawl Stars account begins with the same brawler already unlocked. Who is it?
When Brawl Stars first launched, only two of its brawlers were Legendary rarity — and one of them is a little cactus. Name him.
Most brawlers are earned slowly, but one melee fighter who heals a little with every punch was handed out free as a 2020 holiday gift. Who is he?
Most Supers push, blast, or summon. One genie-themed brawler instead shoots a magic hand that GRABS an enemy and yanks them across the map to him. Who is he?
One fragile robot brawler uses his Super by popping off his OWN HEAD and sending it chasing the nearest enemy like a homing bomb. Who is he?
Almost every brawler can fire while standing still. One shovel-swinging brawler is different: he can't land his basic attack at all unless he DASHES forward. Who is he?
Among the original launch brawlers, one guitar-playing healer can aim his Super at TEAMMATES to restore their health instead of harming enemies. Who is he?
Hiding in a bush usually keeps you safe, but one brawler can summon a bear that SNIFFS OUT and chases enemies out of the bushes. Who summons that bear?
Barley tosses bottles and Dynamike chucks dynamite, both arcing OVER walls to hit hidden enemies. What class do brawlers who lob projectiles over cover belong to?
Brock, Belle, and Bonnie all want to sit far away and pick enemies off from long range, never brawling up close. What class are these snipers?
Gale was the very first brawler of a special Brawl-Pass rarity that Supercell later ABOLISHED, scattering its brawlers into other tiers. What was that rarity called?
Brawl Stars spent over a year in a limited soft launch before opening to everyone. In what year did the game finally release worldwide?
Supercell is based in Finland, but Brawl Stars first opened to the public on iOS in a single other country in 2017. Which country?
Of a brawler's two big kit upgrades — Star Powers (passive) and Gadgets (an active button) — which one was added to the game FIRST?
Most Brawl Stars modes are three-versus-three. Which classic mode instead drops ten players in alone, with no teammates?
In which classic 3v3 mode can you win without defeating a single opponent, just by smashing the enemy's safe?
In Bounty, you earn stars by defeating enemies. How many stars must a team reach to clinch an instant win?
In Gem Grab, a team must hold a certain number of gems through a countdown to win. How many gems?
One of Brawl Stars' most-played modes is a soccer match where you push a ball into a goal. Despite feeling original, it was added the year after global launch. Name it.
An early co-op event called 'Boss Fight' was so demanding it crashed the game and got swapped out. Which surviving three-player co-op event replaced it?
Before 'Power League' and today's 'Ranked,' Brawl Stars' first seasonal competitive ladder had a different name. What was it called?
The first-ever Brawl Stars World Finals was held in Busan, South Korea in 2019. Which team won it?
Which college football program has won more games than any other in the sport's history?
Which school has finished more seasons undefeated than any other program?
Which college has produced the most players taken first overall in the NFL Draft?
Setting aside the modern powerhouses, which school owns the most NCAA-recognized national championships of all time?
Which program has won the most Big 12 football championships?
Since the College Football Playoff began, which team has earned a spot in the bracket the most times?
Which bowl game is the oldest one still played today?

dimension: active coach wins at one school
q: Among coaches still on the sideline, which Big Ten school employs the man who has won more games at a single league program than anyone in conference history?
Which school has produced the most winners of the trophy given to college football's best interior lineman?
Which team currently owns the nation's longest unbroken streak of reaching a bowl game?
The very first Heisman Trophy went to a player from which school — one that later dropped big-time football?
Which school was the first to play its home games on a field that wasn't green?
Which team scored more total points in a single season than any other in the modern game?
Which SEC school set the league's all-time attendance record, packing in a crowd north of 109,000 for a 2004 game?
Setting the Big House aside, which school plays in the second-largest stadium in the country?
Which team became the first undefeated champion of a major conference ever left out of the College Football Playoff?
Which team most recently won the College Football Playoff national championship?
Alabama has gone to one particular bowl more than any other in its history — which one?
Which school has played in the Cotton Bowl more times than any other?
A running back from which school holds the record for the most career rushing yards in major college football?
Counting only the seasons since the year 2000, which program has won the most national championships?
Which program has won more games than any other team that has never won a national championship?
Players from which school have won the Heisman Trophy more times than from any other?
Only one player has ever won the Heisman Trophy twice. What school did he play for?
Which head coach won more games than anyone else in major-college (FBS) football history?
Among coaches who led a major-college team for at least ten years, who has the best career winning percentage?
Which U.S. state is home to more top-division (FBS) football teams than any other?
The most-played rivalry in all of college football is between two small schools in the same state. Which state?
Leaving out the Big Ten, which school plays in the largest football stadium?
After Texas and Oklahoma left in 2024, which school now has the largest stadium in the Big 12?
Which SEC school plays its home games in the smallest town?
The very first college football game was played between two New Jersey schools, 25 players to a side, under soccer-style rules. In which decade?

After a season so deadly that 18 players died, a U.S. president called Harvard, Yale, and Princeton to the White House and pushed the reforms that brought the forward pass and the group that became the NCAA. Who was that president?
In the most lopsided game in college football history, coach John Heisman's Georgia Tech overwhelmed an undermanned Cumberland team. How many points did Georgia Tech score?
The longest winning streak in major college football history was built by Bud Wilkinson's Oklahoma teams in the 1950s, before Notre Dame finally ended it 7–0. How many games long was the streak?
Florida State holds the record for the most seasons in a row reaching a bowl game. About how many straight seasons was the streak?
All-time, which position has produced the most Heisman Trophy winners?
Among the roughly 130 schools in college football's top division, which animal nickname is shared by the most teams?
What is the name of the oldest rivalry trophy in major college football — a plain container one team left behind after a game in 1903?
At one school, players rub a rock for luck and then sprint down a hill into the stadium before every home game. Which school?
Russia is by far the biggest country touching Europe, but most of it sits in Asia. Which country that lies entirely within Europe is the largest by land area?
Most large EU countries use the euro. Which is the most populous EU country that still has its own national currency instead?
Which European river flows through or borders more countries than any other river in the world?
Counting only people in the European Union, which language has the most native speakers?
Which European country has four official languages at the national level?
Among capital cities on the European mainland, which one sits farthest north?
Which country was the most recent to swap out its old currency and start using the euro?
Which European country shares a land border with nine others, the most of any country on the continent?
Set aside tiny non-members like the Vatican and Monaco. Which actual member of the European Union is the smallest by land area?
Which mountain is the highest in Greece, and was said in old stories to be the home of the gods?

Six countries signed the 1951 treaty that started the project that grew into today's European Union. Three were France, Italy, and West Germany. Which small country, now a major EU capital host, was a fourth founder along with Belgium and the Netherlands?
Which lake lying wholly within Europe has the largest surface area?
Europe's biggest lakes mostly sit in Russia, which isn't in the EU. Which is the largest lake that lies inside the European Union?
Germany has the largest economy in the European Union. Which EU country has the second-largest?
Thanks to thousands of inlets and islands, which European country has by far the longest coastline, even though it isn't especially large?
Which EU member is an island nation in the eastern Mediterranean, far from the rest of the bloc and split by a long-running dispute?
England and France fought a famous on-and-off conflict named for about how long it ran. To the nearest hundred years, how long did it actually last?
Which European country holds more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other?
Mount Elbrus in the Caucasus is Europe's highest point, but many leave it off as too far east. Which peak is the highest in the Alps and in western Europe?
Among capital cities on the European mainland, which one sits farthest south?
Set Brazil aside. Which country has won the most men's World Cups, lifting the trophy four times across two different eras?

dimension: first host outside Europe and South America
q: For its first 40 years the World Cup stayed in Europe and South America. Which country finally broke that pattern as the first host on neither continent?

dimension: decade of the single-tournament goal record
q: One player scored 13 goals at a single World Cup — a record untouched ever since. In which decade did he do it?

dimension: most goals by one side in a match
q: What is the most goals one team has ever scored in a single World Cup match — a tally hit only once, in a 1982 rout of El Salvador?
How many career World Cup goals did the all-time men's record holder finish with — a German striker who set the mark at the 2014 tournament?
The World Cup used a 32-team field for many years before 2026's jump to 48. In which decade did the tournament first expand to 32 teams?

dimension: first host eliminated in group stage
q: Hosts almost always survive the group stage. Which country became the first World Cup host ever knocked out in the first round?

dimension: nation that kept the original trophy
q: The original World Cup trophy was awarded permanently to the first nation to win three titles. Which country got to keep it for good?
Which country has reached the most men's World Cup finals while never winning the tournament — losing the final three separate times?
Hosts Brazil needed only a draw in front of nearly 200,000 fans but lost the deciding match to Uruguay. In which decade did this 'Maracanazo' happen?

dimension: deepest African run in the tournament
q: No African team had ever reached a World Cup semifinal until one did in 2022, finishing fourth. Which country was it?

dimension: nation present at every World Cup
q: Which is the only country to have appeared at every single men's World Cup, missing none since 1930?
Germany won World Cups in four different decades. In which decade did it claim its most recent title, beating Argentina in the final?
The 2026 World Cup is spread across three countries. How many host cities are staging matches — a guessable round number?
Which country scored a record 27 goals in a single World Cup, yet still lost the final that year?
Only one men's World Cup final has ever finished scoreless after extra time, forcing a shootout. Which decade saw that final?
Which nation won the very first World Cup, taking the title as host of the inaugural tournament?
The fastest goal in men's World Cup history was scored in just 11 seconds in 2002. Which country's striker scored it?
For decades, every World Cup held outside Europe was won by a South American team. Which European nation finally broke that, winning in South Africa?
In the women's game, one nation towers over the rest with four World Cup titles. Which country is it?
Which country grows more coffee than any other, producing more than a third of the world's supply?
Leaving out South America, which country grows the most coffee in the world?
By share of all meat eaten worldwide, which single type of meat is eaten the most?
Which country produces the most olive oil, making about half the world's supply?
Which country drinks the most tea per person each year?
Which country grows more bananas than any other in the world?
India grows the most bananas but sells almost none abroad. Which country actually exports the most bananas?
Which country drinks the most beer per person, and has topped the list for over 30 years straight?
Which country grows the most wheat in the world?
Which country produces most of the world's vanilla, over 80% of it?
Which country grows over 90% of the world's saffron, the most expensive spice by weight?
Tempura, the fried-batter dish, is famous as Japanese, but which European country's missionaries taught Japan to make it?
Which country grows more cocoa, the bean that chocolate is made from, than any other?
What is the name of the compound in chili peppers that makes them taste hot?
Which country made the most wine in 2024, edging out France?
Which U.S. state makes the most cheese, about a quarter of the country's total?
Which U.S. state grows the most potatoes, roughly a third of the national crop?
Which single topping is the most popular on pizza in the United States?
Despite its Scandinavian-looking name, in which country was the ice cream brand Haagen-Dazs actually created?
How many holes are punched into a single round Ritz cracker?
Of all the courses that have hosted the U.S. Open, which one saw the highest scoring average in a championship — a 1974 event so brutal it earned a nickname for the carnage?
Besides the United States, which country has produced the most men's major championship winners, owed largely to the game's birthplace heritage?
Who shot the lowest single round in PGA Tour history, a 58 at the 2016 Travelers Championship?
New York has hosted the most U.S. Opens. Which state ranks second, thanks to storied courses like Oakmont and Merion?
When golf returned to the Olympics in 2016, who won the men's gold medal — the first awarded in the sport in over a century?
Which course has hosted The Open Championship more times than any other, fitting as the spiritual home of golf?
Counting every staging since 1927, which side has won the Ryder Cup outright the most times overall?
At The Open Championship, the record low score relative to par over 72 holes is how many under — a mark Cameron Smith matched at St Andrews in 2022?
Under the rules of golf, a ball can't weigh more than how many ounces — a limit meant to cap how far it flies?
Who came from ten shots back on the final day to win a major — the largest last-round comeback in major history, at Carnoustie in 1999?
Behind the U.S. and Scotland, which country sits third for most men's major titles — home to stars from Faldo to Rose?
Which course played as the longest in major championship history when it hosted the 2017 U.S. Open in Wisconsin?
Who became the oldest man to win a major, capturing the 2021 PGA Championship at age 50?
Which player tops the PGA Tour's all-time career money list, having earned well over $120 million on tour?
A full regulation 18-hole course is most commonly designed to a par of what number?
Which sparsely populated northern Plains state has the most golf courses per person of any U.S. state?
Outside of the putter, the rules cap a golf club's overall length at how many inches?
Which country, home to Player, Els, and Goosen, ranks fourth all-time in men's major titles despite its modest size?
Florida has the most golf courses of any state. Which large western state ranks second?
Which New York course was the first publicly owned, government-run track to host a U.S. Open, doing so in 2002?
Three brothers split the world by lot — sky, sea, and the dead. The one who drew the dead is a son of Cronus and brother of Zeus, yet he's usually left off the list of the twelve Olympians. Name him.
One Olympian is counted as the newest at the table, taking a throne a gentler goddess gave up to keep the number at twelve. Which god is this late arrival?
He's the only god ever cast out of Olympus who later returned to live there. Hurled from the mountain as an infant, he grew into the gods' master craftsman. Name him.
Among the twelve, one goddess in the most famous account was born from no marriage at all — she rose from sea foam stirred when a Titan's severed flesh fell into the water. Name her.
Of all the mortal-born heroes, only one ended his story by burning away his human half and being welcomed onto Olympus as a true god, married to the cupbearer Hebe. Name him.
Three snake-haired sisters could turn onlookers to stone, but two of them could never die. A hero's whole quest was possible only because the third one could. Name that mortal sister.
A fire-breathing beast — lion in front, goat in the middle, serpent for a tail — was killed from above by a hero riding a winged horse. Name the hero, not the horse.
A wise tutor of heroes, born immortal, was struck by a poisoned arrow whose agony could not kill him. He chose to surrender his deathlessness so another could be freed. Name him.
A wrestling giant forced travelers to fight him and couldn't be beaten on the ground, since contact with his mother renewed his strength. Heracles won only by holding him in the air. Name the giant.
A huntress sworn to virginity outran every suitor in a footrace, until one beat her by tossing golden apples to slow her down. Name her.
Among the famous sinners punished in Tartarus, one is doomed to shove a boulder up a hill forever, watching it roll back each time he nears the top. Name him.
One Olympian alone moves freely among the heavens, the earth, and the land of the dead, escorting souls to the river of the underworld. Name him.

Powerful and feared, this Olympian was nonetheless the most hated of all the gods — even Zeus told him so to his face. A rival deity stood for the disciplined, strategic side of the same domain. Name the hated god.
Dipped as a baby in a magic river, this warrior was unharmable everywhere except the one spot his mother gripped. He died from a single arrow there. Name him.
Winter comes, the Greeks said, from a goddess's grief: her daughter must spend part of each year below because she ate a few pomegranate seeds. Name the grieving mother, not the daughter.
Gods are deathless, yet one ancient story reports a god's death announced across the sea to a passing sailor. This horned god of the wild is the one the tale names as dead. Name him.
A hundred-eyed watchman was set to guard a girl Zeus loved; after Hermes killed him, a goddess saved his many eyes by setting them in the tail of her sacred bird. Name the watchman.
To kill a monster whose direct gaze turned men to stone, this hero never looked at her — he watched her reflection in a polished shield and struck. Name the hero.
One Norse god let a monstrous wolf bite off his hand so the other gods could chain it up. Odin is famous for losing an eye — this was a different god losing a different part. Who lost the hand?

dimension: a god who became a mother
q: Odin's eight-legged horse Sleipnir had an unusual mother: a Norse god who turned into a mare and gave birth to it. Which god was the mother?
To seal the truce ending their war, the Norse gods all spat into a vat, and from that spittle they made a being so wise he could answer any question. Who was made from the gods' spit?
At Ragnarok, the wolf Fenrir swallows Odin. One of Odin's other sons then avenges him by killing Fenrir and lives on into the renewed world. Which son?
Frigg made every thing in the world swear never to harm her son Baldr, but skipped one plant she thought too young to bother. Loki used that plant to kill Baldr. What plant was it?

dimension: the holdout who doomed a rescue
q: Hel agreed to release the dead Baldr if every single thing in the world wept for him. Everything wept except one giantess who refused, dooming him. By what name did this holdout go?

dimension: villain who was once a defender
q: We remember one Egyptian god as the murderer of Osiris and lord of chaos, but in older myth he stood at the prow of the sun god's boat each night, spearing the serpent that tried to stop the sun. Which god?

dimension: ruler vs embalmer of the dead
q: In Egyptian myth the jackal-headed god handles mummies and guides souls, but he is NOT the ruler of the afterlife. Which green-skinned god actually rules the realm of the dead and judges them?
At the weighing of the heart, one god runs the scales and a monster eats the failures, but a different ibis-headed god stands by to write down the verdict. Who is the record-keeper?

dimension: trick that beat the sun god
q: An Egyptian deity made a snake from Ra's own spit, let it bite him, then offered a cure only if Ra revealed his secret name — gaining power over him. Which deity pulled off this trick?

Ra sent a fierce lioness goddess to punish humans, but she went on such a killing spree that the gods flooded the fields with beer dyed red like blood to get her drunk and stop her. Which goddess nearly wiped out humanity?

Many myths tell of someone going to the land of the dead to retrieve a loved one and failing. In the JAPANESE version, a god goes to Yomi for his dead wife, breaks a rule by looking at her rotting body, and flees as she chases him. Which god?
In the AZTEC creation, a feathered-serpent god descends to the land of the dead to gather the bones of past humans, then sounds a hole-less conch to win them. Which god rebuilt humanity from bones?

In a MESOPOTAMIAN myth, a goddess of love and war descends to her sister's underworld; at each of seven gates she must give up one piece of clothing, arriving naked and powerless to be struck dead. Which goddess made this descent?

dimension: first to die rules the dead
q: In the HINDU Vedas, the lord of the dead earned his throne in an unusual way: he was the very first mortal ever to die, and so became the one who rules over all who follow. Which god?
A demon won a boon: he couldn't be killed by man or beast, indoors or out, by day or night, on ground or in air. Vishnu took a form that slipped every clause at once. Which avatar killed him?

dimension: how a god got an animal head
q: One Hindu god guards his mother's door, is beheaded by his own father for blocking him, and is then revived with the head of the first creature found — an elephant. Which god got his head this way?

dimension: the sun hides in a cave
q: In JAPANESE myth, the sun deity is offended by her storm-god brother and hides in a cave, plunging the world into darkness; the gods lure her out with a raucous dance and trick her with a mirror. Which sun deity hid?

Long before Noah, a MESOPOTAMIAN man survived a great flood in a boat, released birds to find land, and was then granted eternal life — later visited by Gilgamesh seeking that same gift. Who was this flood survivor?

An Irish god talked his way into the gods' hall not by one great skill but by claiming mastery of every art at once — and when no single god there could match all of them, the doors opened. Which god earned the title 'Master of All Arts'?
Egypt has its jackal-headed guide of the dead, but the AZTECs had a dog-headed god — twin of the feathered serpent — who guided souls through the underworld. Which god?
After Baldr died, one of Odin's sons borrowed the eight-legged horse Sleipnir and rode nine nights down to Hel to try to ransom him. Which god made the ride?
One Norse god was said to be born of nine mothers, need less sleep than a bird, and hear wool growing on a sheep's back. He guards the rainbow bridge into Asgard. Who is he?
